questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 上託管資料湖，其中包含 Amazon S3 與 Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL 的資料。公司需要涵蓋資料湖所有資料來源的視覺化報告解決方案。只有管理團隊應能完整存取所有視覺化內容，其餘員工只能取得有限存取權。哪一個解決方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company hosts a data lake on AWS. The data lake consists of data in Amazon S3 and Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL. The company needs a reporting solution that provides data visualization and includes all the data sources within the data lake. Only the company's management team should have full access to all the visualizations. The rest of the company should have only limited access. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 Amazon QuickSight 中建立分析，連接所有資料來源並建立資料集。發佈儀表板以視覺化資料，並與適當的 IAM 角色共用儀表板。
A.en: Create an analysis in Amazon QuickSight. Connect all the data sources and create new datasets. Publish dashboards to visualize the data. Share the dashboards with the appropriate IAM roles.

B.zh: 在 Amazon QuickSight 中建立分析，連接所有資料來源並建立資料集。發佈儀表板以視覺化資料，並與適當的使用者和群組共用儀表板。
B.en: Create an analysis in Amazon QuickSight. Connect all the data sources and create new datasets. Publish dashboards to visualize the data. Share the dashboards with the appropriate users and groups.

C.zh: 為 S3 資料建立 AWS Glue 資料表和爬蟲程式，再建立 AWS Glue ETL 任務產生報告。將報告發佈至 S3，並使用 S3 儲存貯體政策限制存取。
C.en: Create an AWS Glue table and crawler for the data in Amazon S3. Create an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job to produce reports. Publish the reports to Amazon S3. Use S3 bucket policies to limit access to the reports.

D.zh: 為 S3 資料建立 AWS Glue 資料表和爬蟲程式，使用 Amazon Athena Federated Query 存取 RDS for PostgreSQL，並以 Athena 產生報告。將報告發佈至 S3，再以儲存貯體政策限制存取。
D.en: Create an AWS Glue table and crawler for the data in Amazon S3. Use Amazon Athena Federated Query to access data within Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL. Generate reports by using Amazon Athena. Publish the reports to Amazon S3. Use S3 bucket policies to limit access to the reports.

A.zh: 錯誤。QuickSight 的內容存取權是授予 QuickSight 使用者與群組，而不是直接將儀表板共用給 IAM 角色；此選項無法正確設定不同受眾的內容權限。
A.en: Incorrect. QuickSight content permissions are granted to QuickSight users and groups rather than by directly sharing dashboards with IAM roles, so this does not correctly define audience access.

B.zh: 正確。QuickSight 可連接 S3 與 RDS 資料集並建立互動式視覺化。以使用者和群組共用儀表板，可讓管理群組取得完整權限，其他群組僅取得所需的有限權限。
B.en: Correct. QuickSight connects to S3 and RDS datasets and creates interactive visualizations. Sharing dashboards with users and groups lets management receive full permissions while other groups receive limited access.

C.zh: 錯誤。Glue ETL 能轉換資料但不是完整的視覺化與儀表板服務，而且方案只處理 S3，沒有納入 RDS PostgreSQL 資料。
C.en: Incorrect. Glue ETL transforms data but is not a complete visualization and dashboard service, and this design only processes S3 without including RDS PostgreSQL.

D.zh: 錯誤。Athena 可查詢多個來源，但把查詢結果發佈到 S3 只提供檔案而非所需的視覺化報告與細緻儀表板共用功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Athena can query multiple sources, but publishing query output to S3 provides files rather than the required visual reporting and granular dashboard sharing.

answer_text.zh: B. 在 Amazon QuickSight 中建立分析，連接所有資料來源並建立資料集。發佈儀表板以視覺化資料，並與適當的使用者和群組共用儀表板。
answer_text.en: B. Create an analysis in Amazon QuickSight. Connect all the data sources and create new datasets. Publish dashboards to visualize the data. Share the dashboards with the appropriate users and groups.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：B（86%），其餘顯示票數合計 8%。討論重點是 QuickSight 原生提供多來源視覺化，且儀表板應依 QuickSight 使用者與群組管理權限。
discussion.en: Community vote: B (86%), with the displayed remaining vote total at 8%. Discussion focused on QuickSight's native multi-source visualization and dashboard permissions managed through QuickSight users and groups.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在實作新的商業應用程式。應用程式在兩個 Amazon EC2 執行個體上執行，並使用 Amazon S3 儲存貯體存放文件。解決方案架構師需要確保 EC2 執行個體可以存取該 S3 儲存貯體。應如何符合此需求？
question_text.en: A company is implementing a new business application. The application runs on two Amazon EC2 instances and uses an Amazon S3 bucket for document storage. A solutions architect needs to ensure that the EC2 instances can access the S3 bucket. What should the solutions architect do to meet this requirement?

A.zh: 建立授予 S3 儲存貯體存取權的 IAM 角色，並將該角色連接至 EC2 執行個體。
A.en: Create an IAM role that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the role to the EC2 instances.

B.zh: 建立授予 S3 儲存貯體存取權的 IAM 政策，並將該政策連接至 EC2 執行個體。
B.en: Create an IAM policy that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the policy to the EC2 instances.

C.zh: 建立授予 S3 儲存貯體存取權的 IAM 群組，並將該群組連接至 EC2 執行個體。
C.en: Create an IAM group that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the group to the EC2 instances.

D.zh: 建立授予 S3 儲存貯體存取權的 IAM 使用者，並將該使用者帳戶連接至 EC2 執行個體。
D.en: Create an IAM user that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the user account to the EC2 instances.

A.zh: 正確。EC2 執行個體設定檔可提供 IAM 角色的臨時憑證，讓兩個執行個體依最低權限存取 S3，無須儲存或輪替長期金鑰。
A.en: Correct. An EC2 instance profile supplies temporary credentials for an IAM role, allowing both instances least-privilege S3 access without stored or rotated long-term keys.

B.zh: 錯誤。IAM 政策必須連接至 IAM 身分，例如角色、使用者或群組，不能直接連接到 EC2 執行個體。
B.en: Incorrect. An IAM policy must be attached to an IAM identity such as a role, user, or group; it cannot be attached directly to an EC2 instance.

C.zh: 錯誤。IAM 群組只能包含 IAM 使用者，不能包含或連接至 EC2 執行個體。
C.en: Incorrect. IAM groups can contain IAM users only and cannot contain or be attached to EC2 instances.

D.zh: 錯誤。IAM 使用者代表人員或固定工作負載身分，不能直接連接至執行個體；在執行個體存放使用者長期憑證也違反最佳實務。
D.en: Incorrect. An IAM user represents a person or fixed workload identity and cannot be attached to an instance. Storing user long-term credentials on EC2 also violates best practices.

answer_text.zh: A. 建立授予 S3 儲存貯體存取權的 IAM 角色，並將該角色連接至 EC2 執行個體。
answer_text.en: A. Create an IAM role that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the role to the EC2 instances.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：A（100%）。討論一致認為 EC2 應使用 IAM 角色取得臨時憑證；政策本身不能直接附加到執行個體，使用者或群組也不是 EC2 的正確身分類型。
discussion.en: Community vote: A (100%). Comments agreed that EC2 should use an IAM role for temporary credentials; a policy alone cannot attach to an instance, and users or groups are not the correct EC2 identity type.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 應用程式開發團隊正在設計一個微服務，將大型影像轉換為較小的壓縮影像。使用者透過 Web 介面上傳影像時，微服務應將原始影像存入 Amazon S3 儲存貯體、以 AWS Lambda 函數處理及壓縮影像，並將壓縮版本存入另一個 S3 儲存貯體。解決方案必須使用耐久且無狀態的元件自動處理影像。哪兩項動作符合需求？（選擇兩項。）
question_text.en: An application development team is designing a microservice that will convert large images to smaller, compressed images. When a user uploads an image through the web interface, the microservice should store the image in an Amazon S3 bucket, process and compress the image with an AWS Lambda function, and store the image in its compressed form in a different S3 bucket. A solutions architect needs to design a solution that uses durable, stateless components to process the images automatically. Which combination of actions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 建立 Amazon SQS 佇列，並設定 S3 儲存貯體在影像上傳時將通知傳送至 SQS 佇列。
A.en: Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Configure the S3 bucket to send a notification to the SQS queue when an image is uploaded to the S3 bucket.

B.zh: 設定 Lambda 函數使用 Amazon SQS 佇列作為叫用來源，並在成功處理 SQS 訊息後刪除佇列中的訊息。
B.en: Configure the Lambda function to use the Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue as the invocation source. When the SQS message is successfully processed, delete the message in the queue.

C.zh: 設定 Lambda 函數監控 S3 儲存貯體的新上傳。偵測到影像後，將檔名寫入記憶體中的文字檔案，以追蹤已處理的影像。
C.en: Configure the Lambda function to monitor the S3 bucket for new uploads. When an uploaded image is detected, write the file name to a text file in memory and use the text file to keep track of the images that were processed.

D.zh: 啟動 Amazon EC2 執行個體監控 Amazon SQS 佇列。佇列出現項目時，將檔名記錄於 EC2 上的文字檔案並叫用 Lambda 函數。
D.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance to monitor an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. When items are added to the queue, log the file name in a text file on the EC2 instance and invoke the Lambda function.

E.zh: 設定 Amazon EventBridge 事件監控 S3 儲存貯體。上傳影像時，將警示傳送至以應用程式擁有者電子郵件為訂閱者的 Amazon SNS 主題，以供進一步處理。
E.en: Configure an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) event to monitor the S3 bucket. When an image is uploaded, send an alert to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic with the application owner's email address for further processing.

A.zh: 正確。S3 事件通知可將每次上傳可靠地送入耐久的 SQS 佇列，解耦上傳與影像處理並在 Lambda 暫時失敗時保留工作。
A.en: Correct. S3 event notifications can place each upload in a durable SQS queue, decoupling ingestion from processing and retaining work during temporary Lambda failures.

B.zh: 正確。Lambda 的 SQS 事件來源映射會輪詢佇列並叫用無狀態函數；成功後移除訊息，失敗訊息則可重試或移至無效信件佇列。
B.en: Correct. A Lambda SQS event source mapping polls the queue and invokes the stateless function. Successful messages are removed, while failures can be retried or sent to a dead-letter queue.

C.zh: 錯誤。Lambda 的本機記憶體與暫存檔案不是跨叫用耐久狀態，且函數不應自行持續輪詢 S3。
C.en: Incorrect. Lambda memory and local temporary files are not durable across invocations, and a function should not continuously poll S3 itself.

D.zh: 錯誤。常駐 EC2 與本機文字檔引入有狀態元件、單點故障及伺服器管理，違反耐久、無狀態設計。
D.en: Incorrect. A persistent EC2 instance and local text file introduce state, a single point of failure, and server management, violating the durable stateless design.

E.zh: 錯誤。電子郵件警示需要人工介入，不能自動觸發壓縮處理，也不是可擴展的工作佇列。
E.en: Incorrect. An email alert requires manual intervention, does not automatically invoke compression, and is not a scalable work queue.

answer_text.zh: A. 建立 Amazon SQS 佇列，並設定 S3 儲存貯體在影像上傳時將通知傳送至 SQS 佇列。；B. 設定 Lambda 函數使用 Amazon SQS 佇列作為叫用來源，並在成功處理 SQS 訊息後刪除佇列中的訊息。
answer_text.en: A. Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Configure the S3 bucket to send a notification to the SQS queue when an image is uploaded to the S3 bucket.; B. Configure the Lambda function to use the Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue as the invocation source. When the SQS message is successfully processed, delete the message in the queue.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：A、B 組合（99%），其他組合合計 1%。討論共識是 S3 通知、SQS 耐久緩衝與 Lambda 無狀態處理構成可重試且解耦的事件驅動管線。
discussion.en: Community vote: A and B together (99%), other combinations 1%. The consensus was that S3 notification, durable SQS buffering, and stateless Lambda processing form a decoupled, retryable event-driven pipeline.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 部署三層式 Web 應用程式，Web 伺服器位於 VPC 公有子網路，應用程式與資料庫伺服器位於同一 VPC 的私有子網路。公司在檢查 VPC 中部署來自 AWS Marketplace 的第三方虛擬防火牆設備，該設備具有可接收 IP 封包的介面。解決方案架構師需要讓所有進入應用程式的流量在抵達 Web 伺服器前通過該設備檢查，並將營運負擔降至最低。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company has a three-tier web application that is deployed on AWS. The web servers are deployed in a public subnet in a VPC. The application servers and database servers are deployed in private subnets in the same VPC. The company has deployed a third-party virtual firewall appliance from AWS Marketplace in an inspection VPC. The appliance is configured with an IP interface that can accept IP packets. A solutions architect needs to integrate the web application with the appliance to inspect all traffic to the application before the traffic reaches the web server. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 在應用程式 VPC 的公有子網路建立 Network Load Balancer，將流量路由至設備進行封包檢查。
A.en: Create a Network Load Balancer in the public subnet of the application's VPC to route the traffic to the appliance for packet inspection.

B.zh: 在應用程式 VPC 的公有子網路建立 Application Load Balancer，將流量路由至設備進行封包檢查。
B.en: Create an Application Load Balancer in the public subnet of the application's VPC to route the traffic to the appliance for packet inspection.

C.zh: 在檢查 VPC 部署傳輸閘道，並設定路由表讓傳入封包通過傳輸閘道。
C.en: Deploy a transit gateway in the inspection VPC. Configure route tables to route the incoming packets through the transit gateway.

D.zh: 在檢查 VPC 部署 Gateway Load Balancer，並建立 Gateway Load Balancer 端點接收傳入封包，再將封包轉送至設備。
D.en: Deploy a Gateway Load Balancer in the inspection VPC. Create a Gateway Load Balancer endpoint to receive the incoming packets and forward the packets to the appliance.

A.zh: 錯誤。NLB 用於分配 TCP、UDP 或 TLS 連線至應用程式目標，不提供第三方虛擬設備所需的透明封包插入、服務鏈與設備健康擴展功能。
A.en: Incorrect. An NLB distributes TCP, UDP, or TLS connections to application targets but does not provide transparent packet insertion, service chaining, and appliance scaling for third-party virtual appliances.

B.zh: 錯誤。ALB 在第 7 層處理 HTTP/HTTPS，無法透明轉送所有 IP 流量或保留虛擬防火牆需要的封包語意。
B.en: Incorrect. An ALB processes HTTP/HTTPS at Layer 7 and cannot transparently forward all IP traffic or preserve packet semantics required by a virtual firewall.

C.zh: 錯誤。傳輸閘道可連接多個 VPC，但單獨使用不會將流量分配至一組防火牆設備，也缺少透明設備插入與健康檢查。
C.en: Incorrect. A transit gateway connects VPCs, but by itself it does not distribute traffic across firewall appliances and lacks transparent appliance insertion and health checking.

D.zh: 正確。Gateway Load Balancer 專為防火牆等虛擬網路設備設計，端點可在 VPC 間透明導引封包，而負載平衡器負責設備健康檢查、擴展與流量分配。
D.en: Correct. Gateway Load Balancer is purpose-built for virtual network appliances such as firewalls. Its endpoints transparently steer packets across VPCs while the load balancer handles appliance health, scaling, and distribution.

answer_text.zh: D. 在檢查 VPC 部署 Gateway Load Balancer，並建立 Gateway Load Balancer 端點接收傳入封包，再將封包轉送至設備。
answer_text.en: D. Deploy a Gateway Load Balancer in the inspection VPC. Create a Gateway Load Balancer endpoint to receive the incoming packets and forward the packets to the appliance.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：D（84%），其餘顯示為 Other。討論認為 Gateway Load Balancer 與其端點是將第三方防火牆透明插入流量路徑的受管模式，營運負擔低於自行以一般負載平衡器或路由元件整合。
discussion.en: Community vote: D (84%), with the remainder displayed as Other. Discussion identified Gateway Load Balancer and its endpoints as the managed pattern for transparently inserting third-party firewalls, with less overhead than integrating ordinary load balancers or routing components.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望提升在同一 AWS 區域內，將大量正式環境資料複製到測試環境的能力。資料位於 Amazon EC2 執行個體的 Amazon EBS 磁碟區。測試副本的修改不得影響正式環境，而且存取資料的軟體需要持續一致的高 I/O 效能。解決方案架構師必須將複製所需時間降至最低。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company wants to improve its ability to clone large amounts of production data into a test environment in the same AWS Region. The data is stored in Amazon EC2 instances on Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Modifications to the cloned data must not affect the production environment. The software that accesses this data requires consistently high I/O performance. A solutions architect needs to minimize the time that is required to clone the production data into the test environment. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 建立正式環境 EBS 磁碟區的快照，並將快照還原到測試環境的 EC2 執行個體儲存體磁碟區。
A.en: Take EBS snapshots of the production EBS volumes. Restore the snapshots onto EC2 instance store volumes in the test environment.

B.zh: 設定正式環境 EBS 磁碟區使用 EBS Multi-Attach。建立正式環境磁碟區快照，並將正式環境 EBS 磁碟區連接至測試環境的 EC2 執行個體。
B.en: Configure the production EBS volumes to use the EBS Multi-Attach feature. Take EBS snapshots of the production EBS volumes. Attach the production EBS volumes to the EC2 instances in the test environment.

C.zh: 建立正式環境 EBS 磁碟區的快照。建立並初始化新的 EBS 磁碟區，將其連接至測試環境 EC2 執行個體後，再從正式環境快照還原磁碟區。
C.en: Take EBS snapshots of the production EBS volumes. Create and initialize new EBS volumes. Attach the new EBS volumes to EC2 instances in the test environment before restoring the volumes from the production EBS snapshots.

D.zh: 建立正式環境 EBS 磁碟區的快照，對快照啟用 EBS 快速快照還原，將快照還原為新的 EBS 磁碟區，再連接至測試環境 EC2 執行個體。
D.en: Take EBS snapshots of the production EBS volumes. Turn on the EBS fast snapshot restore feature on the EBS snapshots. Restore the snapshots into new EBS volumes. Attach the new EBS volumes to EC2 instances in the test environment.

A.zh: 錯誤。EBS 快照只能建立新的 EBS 磁碟區，不能直接還原至暫時性的 EC2 執行個體儲存體。
A.en: Incorrect. EBS snapshots create new EBS volumes and cannot be restored directly onto ephemeral EC2 instance store volumes.

B.zh: 錯誤。將正式環境磁碟區直接 Multi-Attach 到測試執行個體不會建立隔離副本，測試寫入可能改變正式資料；Multi-Attach 也只適用特定磁碟區類型與同一可用區。
B.en: Incorrect. Multi-Attaching the production volume to test instances does not create an isolated copy, so test writes could modify production data. Multi-Attach also has volume-type and same-AZ constraints.

C.zh: 錯誤。一般從快照建立的磁碟區採延遲載入，尚未讀取的區塊第一次存取時可能有較高延遲；選項描述的先建置與初始化流程也不是從快照快速複製的正確順序。
C.en: Incorrect. Ordinary snapshot-restored volumes use lazy loading, so first access to untouched blocks can have higher latency. The described create-and-initialize-before-restore sequence is also not the proper fast clone workflow.

D.zh: 正確。快速快照還原會建立在建立時即完整初始化的 EBS 磁碟區，立即提供其佈建效能，且新磁碟區與正式環境隔離，可安全修改。
D.en: Correct. Fast snapshot restore creates fully initialized EBS volumes that immediately deliver provisioned performance. The new volumes are isolated from production and can be modified safely.

answer_text.zh: D. 建立正式環境 EBS 磁碟區的快照，對快照啟用 EBS 快速快照還原，將快照還原為新的 EBS 磁碟區，再連接至測試環境 EC2 執行個體。
answer_text.en: D. Take EBS snapshots of the production EBS volumes. Turn on the EBS fast snapshot restore feature on the EBS snapshots. Restore the snapshots into new EBS volumes. Attach the new EBS volumes to EC2 instances in the test environment.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：D（94%），其餘顯示票數合計 4%。討論重點是快速快照還原可避免從一般快照建立磁碟區後的首次讀取效能損失，並保留獨立副本。
discussion.en: Community vote: D (94%), with the displayed remaining vote total at 4%. Discussion emphasized that fast snapshot restore avoids first-read performance penalties of ordinary snapshot-created volumes while preserving an independent copy.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家電子商務公司希望在 AWS 推出每日一項特價商品的網站，每項商品販售 24 小時。公司需要在尖峰時段以毫秒級延遲處理每小時數百萬個請求，並將營運負擔降至最低。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: An ecommerce company wants to launch a one-deal-a-day website on AWS. Each day will feature exactly one product on sale for a period of 24 hours. The company wants to be able to handle millions of requests each hour with millisecond latency during peak hours. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon S3 在不同儲存貯體託管整個網站，加入以各 S3 儲存貯體為來源的 CloudFront 分佈，並將訂單資料存入 Amazon S3。
A.en: Use Amazon S3 to host the full website in different S3 buckets. Add Amazon CloudFront distributions. Set the S3 buckets as origins for the distributions. Store the order data in Amazon S3.

B.zh: 將整個網站部署於跨多個可用區的 EC2 Auto Scaling 群組，使用一個 ALB 分配網站流量、另一個 ALB 處理後端 API，並將資料存入 Amazon RDS for MySQL。
B.en: Deploy the full website on Amazon EC2 instances that run in Auto Scaling groups across multiple Availability Zones. Add an Application Load Balancer (ALB) to distribute the website traffic. Add another ALB for the backend APIs. Store the data in Amazon RDS for MySQL.

C.zh: 將整個應用程式遷移至容器並託管於 Amazon EKS，使用 Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler 增減 Pod 以處理流量尖峰，並將資料存入 Amazon RDS for MySQL。
C.en: Migrate the full application to run in containers. Host the containers on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS). Use the Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler to increase and decrease the number of pods to process bursts in traffic. Store the data in Amazon RDS for MySQL.

D.zh: 以 Amazon S3 儲存貯體託管網站靜態內容，部署以該儲存貯體為來源的 CloudFront 分佈。後端 API 使用 Amazon API Gateway 與 AWS Lambda，並將資料存入 Amazon DynamoDB。
D.en: Use an Amazon S3 bucket to host the website's static content. Deploy an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Set the S3 bucket as the origin. Use Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda functions for the backend APIs. Store the data in Amazon DynamoDB.

A.zh: 錯誤。S3 適合靜態網站內容，但不是交易式訂單資料庫，無法提供訂單寫入所需的 API 處理、條件更新與一致性控制。
A.en: Incorrect. S3 is suitable for static site content but not as a transactional order database and does not provide the API processing, conditional updates, and consistency controls needed for orders.

B.zh: 錯誤。EC2、兩個 ALB 與關聯式資料庫可以擴展，但需要容量規劃、修補、Auto Scaling 與資料庫連線管理，營運負擔較高。
B.en: Incorrect. EC2, two ALBs, and a relational database can scale, but they require capacity planning, patching, Auto Scaling, and database connection management, creating higher overhead.

C.zh: 錯誤。EKS 可處理尖峰，但需管理 Kubernetes 叢集、節點、Pod 擴展及 RDS 容量，對此事件型網站並非最低營運負擔。
C.en: Incorrect. EKS can handle bursts but requires managing Kubernetes clusters, nodes, pod scaling, and RDS capacity, so it is not the lowest-overhead design for this event-driven site.

D.zh: 正確。CloudFront 與 S3 能以低延遲大規模提供靜態內容；API Gateway、Lambda 和 DynamoDB 都是受管且可自動擴展的服務，適合突發的數百萬請求。
D.en: Correct. CloudFront and S3 serve static content globally at low latency, while API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB are managed, automatically scaling services suitable for millions of bursty requests.

answer_text.zh: D. 以 Amazon S3 儲存貯體託管網站靜態內容，部署以該儲存貯體為來源的 CloudFront 分佈。後端 API 使用 Amazon API Gateway 與 AWS Lambda，並將資料存入 Amazon DynamoDB。
answer_text.en: D. Use an Amazon S3 bucket to host the website's static content. Deploy an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Set the S3 bucket as the origin. Use Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda functions for the backend APIs. Store the data in Amazon DynamoDB.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：D（100%）。討論一致支持以 CloudFront/S3 加上 API Gateway、Lambda、DynamoDB 的無伺服器架構，在自動擴展與毫秒延遲下把伺服器及叢集管理降至最低。
discussion.en: Community vote: D (100%). Comments unanimously supported the serverless CloudFront/S3 plus API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB architecture for automatic scaling, millisecond latency, and minimal server or cluster management.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師正在使用 Amazon S3 設計數位媒體應用程式的儲存架構。媒體檔案必須能承受一個可用區遺失。部分檔案經常存取，其他檔案則以不可預測的模式很少存取。架構師必須將媒體檔案的儲存與擷取成本降至最低。哪一個儲存選項符合需求？
question_text.en: A solutions architect is using Amazon S3 to design the storage architecture of a new digital media application. The media files must be resilient to the loss of an Availability Zone. Some files are accessed frequently while other files are rarely accessed in an unpredictable pattern. The solutions architect must minimize the costs of storing and retrieving the media files. Which storage option meets these requirements?

A.zh: S3 Standard
A.en: S3 Standard

B.zh: S3 Intelligent-Tiering
B.en: S3 Intelligent-Tiering

C.zh: S3 Standard-Infrequent Access（S3 Standard-IA）
C.en: S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA)

D.zh: S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access（S3 One Zone-IA）
D.en: S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA)

A.zh: 錯誤。這個標準儲存類別可跨多個可用區提供恢復能力，但所有物件都按常用存取層計費，無法針對不可預測的冷資料自動降低儲存成本。
A.en: Incorrect. This standard storage class is resilient across Availability Zones but charges all objects at the frequent-access tier and cannot automatically reduce storage cost for unpredictably cold data.

B.zh: 正確。Intelligent-Tiering 跨至少三個可用區儲存資料，並依實際存取模式自動將物件移至合適層級，對不可預測模式可降低成本且沒有存取層取回費。
B.en: Correct. Intelligent-Tiering stores data across at least three Availability Zones and automatically moves objects to appropriate tiers based on actual access, lowering cost for unpredictable patterns without retrieval fees for its access tiers.

C.zh: 錯誤。Standard-IA 能承受可用區故障，但每次擷取收費且有最低儲存期間；由於哪些檔案會變冷不可預測，手動選擇此層可能增加頻繁存取檔案的成本。
C.en: Incorrect. Standard-IA is resilient to an AZ failure but charges for retrieval and has a minimum storage duration. With unpredictable access, placing frequently used files there can increase cost.

D.zh: 錯誤。One Zone-IA 只將資料存於單一可用區，無法符合遺失一個可用區後仍具恢復能力的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. One Zone-IA stores data in one Availability Zone and therefore cannot meet the requirement to survive the loss of an AZ.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：B（99%），其他選項合計 1%。討論共識是存取模式不可預測時應由 Intelligent-Tiering 自動分層，同時維持多可用區恢復能力。
discussion.en: Community vote: B (99%), other choices 1%. The consensus was to use Intelligent-Tiering for automatic tiering under unpredictable access while retaining multi-AZ resiliency.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 Amazon S3 Standard 儲存備份檔案。檔案在第一個月經常存取，但一個月後便不再存取。公司必須無限期保留檔案。哪一個儲存方案最具成本效益？
question_text.en: A company is storing backup files by using Amazon S3 Standard storage. The files are accessed frequently for 1 month. However, the files are not accessed after 1 month. The company must keep the files indefinitely. Which storage solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 設定 S3 Intelligent-Tiering 自動遷移物件。
A.en: Configure S3 Intelligent-Tiering to automatically migrate objects.

B.zh: 建立 S3 生命週期設定，在一個月後將物件從 S3 Standard 轉換至 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
B.en: Create an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition objects from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 month.

C.zh: 建立 S3 生命週期設定，在一個月後將物件從 S3 Standard 轉換至 S3 Standard-Infrequent Access。
C.en: Create an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition objects from S3 Standard to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) after 1 month.

D.zh: 建立 S3 生命週期設定，在一個月後將物件從 S3 Standard 轉換至 S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access。
D.en: Create an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition objects from S3 Standard to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA) after 1 month.

A.zh: 錯誤。Intelligent-Tiering 適合未知或變動的存取模式，但本題已知一個月後完全不再存取；監控費用與較昂貴的非封存層不如直接轉入最便宜的封存層。
A.en: Incorrect. Intelligent-Tiering suits unknown or changing access, but this pattern is known to become inactive after one month. Monitoring and non-archive tiers are less economical than a direct move to the lowest-cost archive.

B.zh: 正確。生命週期規則可在常用期結束後自動轉至 Deep Archive；此層針對長期、幾乎不擷取且必須保留的資料提供最低儲存成本。
B.en: Correct. A lifecycle rule automatically moves files after their active month to Deep Archive, which provides the lowest storage cost for long-lived data that is almost never retrieved.

C.zh: 錯誤。Standard-IA 的儲存費低於 Standard，但仍高於 Deep Archive，適合偶爾需要快速擷取的資料；題目明確表示一個月後不再存取。
C.en: Incorrect. Standard-IA costs less than Standard but more than Deep Archive and suits data needing occasional rapid retrieval. The files are explicitly never accessed after one month.

D.zh: 錯誤。One Zone-IA 仍比 Deep Archive 昂貴，且只存於單一可用區，對必須無限期保留的備份降低了恢復能力。
D.en: Incorrect. One Zone-IA still costs more than Deep Archive and stores data in one AZ, reducing resiliency for backups that must be retained indefinitely.

answer_text.zh: B. 建立 S3 生命週期設定，在一個月後將物件從 S3 Standard 轉換至 S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
answer_text.en: B. Create an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition objects from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 month.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：B（97%），其餘顯示票數合計 2%。討論指出存取模式已知且資料要永久保留，因此生命週期轉入最低成本的 Deep Archive 比自動分層或 IA 類別更合適。
discussion.en: Community vote: B (97%), with the displayed remaining vote total at 2%. Discussion noted that the access pattern is known and retention is indefinite, making lifecycle transition to lowest-cost Deep Archive preferable to automatic or IA tiers.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在最新帳單中發現 Amazon EC2 成本增加。計費團隊注意到數個 EC2 執行個體出現不需要的垂直擴展。解決方案架構師需要建立圖表比較最近兩個月的 EC2 成本，並深入分析以找出垂直擴展的根本原因。應如何以最少的營運負擔產生這些資訊？
question_text.en: A company observes an increase in Amazon EC2 costs in its most recent bill. The billing team notices unwanted vertical scaling of instance types for a couple of EC2 instances. A solutions architect needs to create a graph comparing the last 2 months of EC2 costs and perform an in-depth analysis to identify the root cause of the vertical scaling. How should the solutions architect generate the information with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Budgets 建立預算報告，並依執行個體類型比較 EC2 成本。
A.en: Use AWS Budgets to create a budget report and compare EC2 costs based on instance types.

B.zh: 使用 Cost Explorer 的精細篩選功能，依執行個體類型深入分析 EC2 成本。
B.en: Use Cost Explorer's granular filtering feature to perform an in-depth analysis of EC2 costs based on instance types.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Billing and Cost Management 儀表板的圖表，依執行個體類型比較最近兩個月的 EC2 成本。
C.en: Use graphs from the AWS Billing and Cost Management dashboard to compare EC2 costs based on instance types for the last 2 months.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Cost and Usage Reports 產生報告並傳送至 Amazon S3，再以 Amazon QuickSight 讀取 S3，依執行個體類型建立互動式圖表。
D.en: Use AWS Cost and Usage Reports to create a report and send it to an Amazon S3 bucket. Use Amazon QuickSight with Amazon S3 as a source to generate an interactive graph based on instance types.

A.zh: 錯誤。AWS Budgets 主要用於設定成本或用量門檻、預測與警示，不是依執行個體類型進行歷史根本原因分析的工具。
A.en: Incorrect. AWS Budgets primarily sets cost or usage thresholds, forecasts, and alerts; it is not the tool for historical root-cause analysis by instance type.

B.zh: 正確。Cost Explorer 內建歷史圖表、期間比較、群組與精細篩選，可直接依服務、用量類型或執行個體相關維度分析成本，無須建立資料管線。
B.en: Correct. Cost Explorer provides built-in historical graphs, period comparisons, grouping, and granular filters to analyze costs by service, usage type, and instance-related dimensions without a data pipeline.

C.zh: 錯誤。Billing 儀表板提供高階成本摘要，但其篩選與深入切分能力不足以有效找出特定執行個體類型垂直擴展的根因。
C.en: Incorrect. The Billing dashboard provides high-level cost summaries but lacks the granular filtering and drill-down needed to identify the root cause of vertical scaling by instance type.

D.zh: 錯誤。CUR 搭配 S3 與 QuickSight 可做高度客製化分析，但需要設定報告、儲存、資料集及視覺化，營運負擔高於直接使用 Cost Explorer。
D.en: Incorrect. CUR with S3 and QuickSight enables highly customized analysis but requires report delivery, storage, datasets, and visualization setup, adding more overhead than Cost Explorer.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 Cost Explorer 的精細篩選功能，依執行個體類型深入分析 EC2 成本。
answer_text.en: B. Use Cost Explorer's granular filtering feature to perform an in-depth analysis of EC2 costs based on instance types.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：B（66%）、C（25%），其餘顯示票數合計 10%。雖有少數支持 Billing 儀表板，最終判斷是 Cost Explorer 才具備題目所需的精細篩選與深入分析，又不需自行建立報表管線。
discussion.en: Community vote: B (66%), C (25%), with the displayed remaining vote total at 10%. Although a minority favored the Billing dashboard, Cost Explorer provides the required granular filters and deep analysis without a custom reporting pipeline.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在設計應用程式。應用程式使用 AWS Lambda 函數透過 Amazon API Gateway 接收資訊，並將資訊存入 Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL 資料庫。在概念驗證階段，公司必須大幅提高 Lambda 配額才能處理要載入資料庫的大量資料。解決方案架構師需要提出新設計，以改善可擴展性並將設定工作降至最低。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company is designing an application. The application uses an AWS Lambda function to receive information through Amazon API Gateway and to store the information in an Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL database. During the proof-of-concept stage, the company has to increase the Lambda quotas significantly to handle the high volumes of data that the company needs to load into the database. A solutions architect must recommend a new design to improve scalability and minimize the configuration effort. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 將 Lambda 函數程式碼重構為在 Amazon EC2 上執行的 Apache Tomcat 程式碼，並使用原生 JDBC 驅動程式連線資料庫。
A.en: Refactor the Lambda function code to Apache Tomcat code that runs on Amazon EC2 instances. Connect the database by using native Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) drivers.

B.zh: 將平台從 Aurora 改為 Amazon DynamoDB，佈建 DynamoDB Accelerator 叢集，並使用 DAX 用戶端 SDK 將現有 DynamoDB API 呼叫指向 DAX。
B.en: Change the platform from Aurora to Amazon DynamoDB. Provision a DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) cluster. Use the DAX client SDK to point the existing DynamoDB API calls at the DAX cluster.

C.zh: 設定兩個 Lambda 函數，一個接收資訊、另一個將資訊載入資料庫，並使用 Amazon SNS 整合兩個函數。
C.en: Set up two Lambda functions. Configure one function to receive the information. Configure the other function to load the information into the database. Integrate the Lambda functions by using Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS).

D.zh: 設定兩個 Lambda 函數，一個接收資訊、另一個將資訊載入資料庫，並使用 Amazon SQS 佇列整合兩個函數。
D.en: Set up two Lambda functions. Configure one function to receive the information. Configure the other function to load the information into the database. Integrate the Lambda functions by using an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.

A.zh: 錯誤。改寫為 EC2 上的 Tomcat 會增加伺服器、擴展、修補與高可用性管理，設定工作遠高於使用受管的事件服務。
A.en: Incorrect. Rewriting for Tomcat on EC2 adds server, scaling, patching, and high-availability management, creating far more configuration work than managed event services.

B.zh: 錯誤。更換成 DynamoDB 與 DAX 需要重新設計資料模型和應用程式；DAX 主要加速讀取，不能解決大量寫入 Aurora 所造成的 Lambda 尖峰。
B.en: Incorrect. Moving to DynamoDB and DAX requires a new data model and application changes. DAX primarily accelerates reads and does not solve Lambda bursts while loading Aurora.

C.zh: 錯誤。SNS 能扇出推送事件，但不提供與 SQS 相同的耐久積壓緩衝與取用者節流控制；資料庫處理速度跟不上時仍可能造成大量同時叫用。
C.en: Incorrect. SNS can fan out pushed events but does not provide the same durable backlog and consumer throttling controls as SQS. Concurrent invocations can still surge when database processing lags.

D.zh: 正確。SQS 會耐久緩衝大量輸入並解耦接收與資料庫寫入。第二個 Lambda 可依批次與保留並行量控制消費速率，吸收尖峰且只需少量設定。
D.en: Correct. SQS durably buffers high-volume input and decouples ingestion from database writes. The second Lambda can consume in batches with controlled concurrency, absorbing bursts with little configuration.

answer_text.zh: D. 設定兩個 Lambda 函數，一個接收資訊、另一個將資訊載入資料庫，並使用 Amazon SQS 佇列整合兩個函數。
answer_text.en: D. Set up two Lambda functions. Configure one function to receive the information. Configure the other function to load the information into the database. Integrate the Lambda functions by using an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：D（99%），其他選項合計 1%。討論共識是 SQS 能在接收層與 Aurora 寫入層之間提供耐久緩衝與背壓，避免以大量 Lambda 並行直接壓垮資料庫。
discussion.en: Community vote: D (99%), other choices 1%. The consensus was that SQS provides durable buffering and backpressure between ingestion and Aurora writes, avoiding massive Lambda concurrency directly overwhelming the database.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要檢查其 AWS 雲端部署，以確保 Amazon S3 儲存貯體沒有未經授權的組態變更。解決方案架構師應如何達成此目標？
question_text.en: A company needs to review its AWS Cloud deployment to ensure that its Amazon S3 buckets do not have unauthorized configuration changes. What should a solutions architect do to accomplish this goal?

A.zh: 啟用 AWS Config 並設定適當的規則。
A.en: Turn on AWS Config with the appropriate rules.

B.zh: 啟用 AWS Trusted Advisor 並設定適當的檢查。
B.en: Turn on AWS Trusted Advisor with the appropriate checks.

C.zh: 啟用 Amazon Inspector 並設定適當的評估範本。
C.en: Turn on Amazon Inspector with the appropriate assessment template.

D.zh: 啟用 Amazon S3 伺服器存取日誌，並設定 Amazon EventBridge。
D.en: Turn on Amazon S3 server access logging. Configure Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events).

A.zh: 正確。AWS Config 會持續記錄資源組態歷史並以受管或自訂規則評估 S3 是否符合政策，可偵測及追溯未授權變更。
A.en: Correct. AWS Config continuously records resource configuration history and evaluates S3 against managed or custom rules, enabling detection and tracing of unauthorized changes.

B.zh: 錯誤。Trusted Advisor 提供特定成本、效能、配額、容錯與安全最佳實務檢查，但不是完整的 S3 組態變更歷史與規則合規引擎。
B.en: Incorrect. Trusted Advisor offers selected cost, performance, quota, fault-tolerance, and security checks but is not a complete S3 configuration history and rule-compliance engine.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon Inspector 評估運算工作負載的軟體漏洞與網路暴露，不負責監控 S3 儲存貯體組態變更。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon Inspector assesses software vulnerabilities and network exposure in compute workloads; it does not monitor S3 bucket configuration changes.

D.zh: 錯誤。S3 伺服器存取日誌記錄物件與儲存貯體請求，不提供資源組態時間軸或自動判定組態是否合規；僅加上 EventBridge 仍需自建完整邏輯。
D.en: Incorrect. S3 server access logs record bucket and object requests, not a configuration timeline or automatic compliance evaluation. EventBridge alone would still require custom logic.

answer_text.zh: A. 啟用 AWS Config 並設定適當的規則。
answer_text.en: A. Turn on AWS Config with the appropriate rules.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：A（97%），其餘顯示票數合計 2%。討論指出 AWS Config 的核心用途就是記錄組態變更並透過規則持續評估合規性。
discussion.en: Community vote: A (97%), with the displayed remaining vote total at 2%. Discussion noted that AWS Config is specifically designed to record configuration changes and continuously evaluate compliance through rules.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司即將推出新應用程式，並會在 Amazon CloudWatch 儀表板顯示應用程式指標。產品經理需要定期存取此儀表板，但沒有 AWS 帳戶。解決方案架構師必須依最低權限原則提供存取。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company is launching a new application and will display application metrics on an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard. The company's product manager needs to access this dashboard periodically. The product manager does not have an AWS account. A solutions architect must provide access to the product manager by following the principle of least privilege. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 從 CloudWatch 主控台共用儀表板，輸入產品經理的電子郵件地址並完成共用步驟，再提供儀表板的可共用連結。
A.en: Share the dashboard from the CloudWatch console. Enter the product manager's email address, and complete the sharing steps. Provide a shareable link for the dashboard to the product manager.

B.zh: 專為產品經理建立 IAM 使用者，連接 CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess AWS 受管政策，共用登入憑證及正確儀表板的瀏覽器網址。
B.en: Create an IAM user specifically for the product manager. Attach the CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess AWS managed policy to the user. Share the new login credentials with the product manager. Share the browser URL of the correct dashboard with the product manager.

C.zh: 為公司員工建立共用 IAM 使用者並連接 ViewOnlyAccess AWS 受管政策。將登入憑證交給產品經理，請其進入 CloudWatch 主控台尋找儀表板。
C.en: Create an IAM user for the company's employees. Attach the ViewOnlyAccess AWS managed policy to the IAM user. Share the new login credentials with the product manager. Ask the product manager to navigate to the CloudWatch console and locate the dashboard by name in the Dashboards section.

D.zh: 在公有子網路部署堡壘伺服器。產品經理需要儀表板時啟動伺服器並共用 RDP 憑證，使用已快取適當 AWS 憑證且預設開啟儀表板網址的瀏覽器。
D.en: Deploy a bastion server in a public subnet. When the product manager requires access to the dashboard, start the server and share the RDP credentials. On the bastion server, ensure that the browser is configured to open the dashboard URL with cached AWS credentials that have appropriate permissions to view the dashboard.

A.zh: 正確。CloudWatch 儀表板共用可透過電子郵件邀請與專用連結讓沒有 AWS 帳戶的對象只查看指定儀表板，比授予主控台權限更符合最低權限。
A.en: Correct. CloudWatch dashboard sharing uses an email invitation and dedicated link so a person without an AWS account can view only the specified dashboard, providing less privilege than console access.

B.zh: 錯誤。CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess 會授予產品經理讀取廣泛 CloudWatch 資源的主控台權限，範圍超過只查看單一儀表板，且還需管理 IAM 憑證。
B.en: Incorrect. CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess grants console visibility across broad CloudWatch resources, exceeding access to one dashboard and requiring IAM credential management.

C.zh: 錯誤。共用 IAM 使用者違反不可共用憑證的安全實務，而 ViewOnlyAccess 涵蓋大量 AWS 服務，權限遠超需求。
C.en: Incorrect. A shared IAM user violates the practice of unique credentials, and ViewOnlyAccess spans many AWS services, far exceeding the requirement.

D.zh: 錯誤。堡壘主機、RDP 與快取憑證帶來伺服器管理與憑證洩漏風險，且授權邊界不如儀表板共用精確。
D.en: Incorrect. A bastion host, RDP, and cached credentials add server management and credential exposure risk, with a less precise authorization boundary than dashboard sharing.

answer_text.zh: A. 從 CloudWatch 主控台共用儀表板，輸入產品經理的電子郵件地址並完成共用步驟，再提供儀表板的可共用連結。
answer_text.en: A. Share the dashboard from the CloudWatch console. Enter the product manager's email address, and complete the sharing steps. Provide a shareable link for the dashboard to the product manager.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：A（72%）、B（27%）、其他 1%。雖有少數主張建立 IAM 使用者，但直接共用 CloudWatch 儀表板只暴露指定內容，無需 AWS 帳戶，最符合最低權限。
discussion.en: Community vote: A (72%), B (27%), other choices 1%. Although a minority favored an IAM user, direct CloudWatch dashboard sharing exposes only the specified content, needs no AWS account, and best follows least privilege.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在將應用程式遷移至 AWS，應用程式分布在不同帳戶，並使用 AWS Organizations 集中管理。安全團隊需要跨所有公司帳戶的單一登入解決方案，同時必須繼續在內部部署的自管 Microsoft Active Directory 管理使用者與群組。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company is migrating applications to AWS. The applications are deployed in different accounts. The company manages the accounts centrally by using AWS Organizations. The company's security team needs a single sign-on (SSO) solution across all the company's accounts. The company must continue managing the users and groups in its on-premises self-managed Microsoft Active Directory. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 從 AWS SSO 主控台啟用 AWS Single Sign-On，並透過 AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory 建立單向樹系信任或單向網域信任，連接自管 Microsoft Active Directory 與 AWS SSO。
A.en: Enable AWS Single Sign-On (AWS SSO) from the AWS SSO console. Create a one-way forest trust or a one-way domain trust to connect the company's self-managed Microsoft Active Directory with AWS SSO by using AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory.

B.zh: 從 AWS SSO 主控台啟用 AWS Single Sign-On，並透過 AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory 建立雙向樹系信任，連接自管 Microsoft Active Directory 與 AWS SSO。
B.en: Enable AWS Single Sign-On (AWS SSO) from the AWS SSO console. Create a two-way forest trust to connect the company's self-managed Microsoft Active Directory with AWS SSO by using AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Directory Service，並與公司的自管 Microsoft Active Directory 建立雙向信任關係。
C.en: Use AWS Directory Service. Create a two-way trust relationship with the company's self-managed Microsoft Active Directory.

D.zh: 在內部部署環境部署身分提供者，並從 AWS SSO 主控台啟用 AWS Single Sign-On。
D.en: Deploy an identity provider (IdP) on premises. Enable AWS Single Sign-On (AWS SSO) from the AWS SSO console.

A.zh: 錯誤。此整合情境需要 AWS Managed Microsoft AD 與內部部署樹系之間受支援的雙向樹系信任；單向信任不足以提供題目所需的使用者與群組整合。
A.en: Incorrect. This integration requires a supported two-way forest trust between AWS Managed Microsoft AD and the on-premises forest. A one-way trust is insufficient for the required user and group integration.

B.zh: 正確。AWS IAM Identity Center（原 AWS SSO）可與 AWS Organizations 整合，在多帳戶提供集中登入；透過 AWS Managed Microsoft AD 的雙向樹系信任，可繼續在內部部署 AD 管理使用者與群組。
B.en: Correct. AWS IAM Identity Center, formerly AWS SSO, integrates with AWS Organizations for centralized multi-account access. A two-way forest trust through AWS Managed Microsoft AD allows users and groups to remain managed in the on-premises AD.

C.zh: 錯誤。只有 Directory Service 信任關係沒有啟用及設定 AWS SSO，無法提供跨 Organizations 帳戶的單一登入入口與權限指派。
C.en: Incorrect. A Directory Service trust alone does not enable or configure AWS SSO, so it does not provide the multi-account sign-in portal and permission assignments.

D.zh: 錯誤。自建內部部署 IdP 需要額外部署與維護，而且選項沒有說明如何將既有 AD 使用者與群組正確整合為 AWS SSO 身分來源。
D.en: Incorrect. A custom on-premises IdP adds deployment and maintenance, and the option does not establish how the existing AD users and groups become the AWS SSO identity source.

answer_text.zh: B. 從 AWS SSO 主控台啟用 AWS Single Sign-On，並透過 AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory 建立雙向樹系信任，連接自管 Microsoft Active Directory 與 AWS SSO。
answer_text.en: B. Enable AWS Single Sign-On (AWS SSO) from the AWS SSO console. Create a two-way forest trust to connect the company's self-managed Microsoft Active Directory with AWS SSO by using AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：B（69%）、A（27%）、其他 3%。爭議集中於信任方向；依題目官方架構，AWS Managed Microsoft AD 搭配雙向樹系信任，才能讓 AWS SSO 使用仍由內部部署 AD 管理的身分。
discussion.en: Community vote: B (69%), A (27%), other choices 3%. Debate focused on trust direction; in the official architecture, AWS Managed Microsoft AD with a two-way forest trust lets AWS SSO use identities that remain managed in the on-premises AD.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司提供使用 UDP 連線的 Voice over Internet Protocol 服務。服務由 Auto Scaling 群組中的 Amazon EC2 執行個體組成，並部署於多個 AWS 區域。公司需要將使用者路由至延遲最低的區域，並需要區域間自動容錯移轉。哪一個方案符合需求？
question_text.en: A company provides a Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) service that uses UDP connections. The service consists of Amazon EC2 instances that run in an Auto Scaling group. The company has deployments across multiple AWS Regions. The company needs to route users to the Region with the lowest latency. The company also needs automated failover between Regions. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在每個區域部署 Network Load Balancer 及相關目標群組，將目標群組與 Auto Scaling 群組關聯，並將每個 NLB 作為 AWS Global Accelerator 端點。
A.en: Deploy a Network Load Balancer (NLB) and an associated target group. Associate the target group with the Auto Scaling group. Use the NLB as an AWS Global Accelerator endpoint in each Region.

B.zh: 在每個區域部署 Application Load Balancer 及相關目標群組，將目標群組與 Auto Scaling 群組關聯，並將每個 ALB 作為 AWS Global Accelerator 端點。
B.en: Deploy an Application Load Balancer (ALB) and an associated target group. Associate the target group with the Auto Scaling group. Use the ALB as an AWS Global Accelerator endpoint in each Region.

C.zh: 部署 NLB 及相關目標群組並與 Auto Scaling 群組關聯。建立指向各 NLB 別名的 Route 53 延遲記錄，再建立以該延遲記錄為來源的 CloudFront 分佈。
C.en: Deploy a Network Load Balancer (NLB) and an associated target group. Associate the target group with the Auto Scaling group. Create an Amazon Route 53 latency record that points to aliases for each NLB. Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution that uses the latency record as an origin.

D.zh: 部署 ALB 及相關目標群組並與 Auto Scaling 群組關聯。建立指向各 ALB 別名的 Route 53 加權記錄，再建立以該加權記錄為來源的 CloudFront 分佈。
D.en: Deploy an Application Load Balancer (ALB) and an associated target group. Associate the target group with the Auto Scaling group. Create an Amazon Route 53 weighted record that points to aliases for each ALB. Deploy an Amazon CloudFront distribution that uses the weighted record as an origin.

A.zh: 正確。NLB 支援 UDP 並可將流量分配到 Auto Scaling 執行個體；Global Accelerator 會透過 AWS 全球網路將用戶端導向健康且延遲最佳的區域端點，並自動容錯移轉。
A.en: Correct. NLB supports UDP and distributes traffic to Auto Scaling instances. Global Accelerator uses the AWS global network to direct clients to the healthy, lowest-latency regional endpoint and automatically fails over.

B.zh: 錯誤。ALB 只支援 HTTP 與 HTTPS 等第 7 層流量，不支援 VoIP 所使用的 UDP。
B.en: Incorrect. ALB handles Layer 7 protocols such as HTTP and HTTPS and does not support the UDP traffic used by VoIP.

C.zh: 錯誤。NLB 與 Route 53 延遲路由可處理區域選擇，但 CloudFront 是 HTTP/HTTPS 內容傳遞網路，不會代理一般 UDP VoIP 流量。
C.en: Incorrect. NLB and Route 53 latency routing can select Regions, but CloudFront is an HTTP/HTTPS content delivery network and does not proxy general UDP VoIP traffic.

D.zh: 錯誤。ALB 與 CloudFront 都不支援此一般 UDP 工作負載，而且加權記錄不會自動選擇延遲最低的區域。
D.en: Incorrect. Neither ALB nor CloudFront supports this general UDP workload, and weighted records do not automatically choose the lowest-latency Region.

answer_text.zh: A. 在每個區域部署 Network Load Balancer 及相關目標群組，將目標群組與 Auto Scaling 群組關聯，並將每個 NLB 作為 AWS Global Accelerator 端點。
answer_text.en: A. Deploy a Network Load Balancer (NLB) and an associated target group. Associate the target group with the Auto Scaling group. Use the NLB as an AWS Global Accelerator endpoint in each Region.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：A（82%），其餘顯示為 Other。討論重點是 UDP 排除 ALB 與 CloudFront，而 Global Accelerator 搭配各區域 NLB 可同時提供最低延遲路由、健康檢查與快速跨區域容錯移轉。
discussion.en: Community vote: A (82%), with the remainder displayed as Other. Discussion noted that UDP rules out ALB and CloudFront, while Global Accelerator with regional NLBs provides lowest-latency routing, health checks, and fast inter-Region failover.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一個開發團隊每月在已啟用 Performance Insights 的一般用途 Amazon RDS for MySQL 資料庫執行個體上執行資源密集型測試。測試每月執行一次、持續 48 小時，也是唯一使用該資料庫的程序。團隊希望在不降低資料庫執行個體運算與記憶體規格的情況下降低測試成本。哪一個方案最具成本效益？
question_text.en: A development team runs monthly resource-intensive tests on its general purpose Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance with Performance Insights enabled. The testing lasts for 48 hours once a month and is the only process that uses the database. The team wants to reduce the cost of running the tests without reducing the compute and memory attributes of the DB instance. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 測試完成後停止資料庫執行個體，需要時再重新啟動。
A.en: Stop the DB instance when tests are completed. Restart the DB instance when required.

B.zh: 對資料庫執行個體使用 Auto Scaling 政策，在測試完成時自動擴展。
B.en: Use an Auto Scaling policy with the DB instance to automatically scale when tests are completed.

C.zh: 測試完成後建立快照、終止資料庫執行個體，並在需要時還原快照。
C.en: Create a snapshot when tests are completed. Terminate the DB instance and restore the snapshot when required.

D.zh: 測試完成後將資料庫執行個體修改為低容量執行個體，需要時再改回原規格。
D.en: Modify the DB instance to a low-capacity instance when tests are completed. Modify the DB instance again when required.

A.zh: 錯誤。RDS 資料庫執行個體最多只能連續停止 7 天，之後會自動啟動；每月只使用 48 小時時，仍會產生其餘多週的運算費用。
A.en: Incorrect. An RDS DB instance can remain stopped for only seven consecutive days before it is automatically restarted. A database used for 48 hours monthly would still accrue compute charges for the remaining weeks.

B.zh: 錯誤。一般 RDS 資料庫執行個體沒有依排程自動縮放執行個體類別的 Auto Scaling 政策；RDS 儲存體自動擴展也不會停止運算費用。
B.en: Incorrect. A standard RDS DB instance has no Auto Scaling policy that changes its instance class on this schedule. RDS storage autoscaling also does not eliminate compute charges.

C.zh: 正確。快照可耐久保存資料與組態，刪除執行個體後不再支付資料庫運算費用；下次測試時可從快照以相同執行個體規格還原。
C.en: Correct. A snapshot durably preserves data and configuration, and deleting the instance removes database compute charges. It can be restored with the same instance class for the next test.

D.zh: 錯誤。縮小執行個體會直接降低運算與記憶體規格，違反題目限制，而且每月變更類別也會造成停機與管理工作。
D.en: Incorrect. Downsizing directly reduces compute and memory attributes, violating the requirement, and monthly class changes add downtime and management.

answer_text.zh: C. 測試完成後建立快照、終止資料庫執行個體，並在需要時還原快照。
answer_text.en: C. Create a snapshot when tests are completed. Terminate the DB instance and restore the snapshot when required.

discussion.zh: 社群投票：C（81%），其餘顯示為 Other。討論指出停止 RDS 只有 7 天上限，因此月度閒置期間最省成本的方式是保留快照並刪除運算資源，需要時以原規格還原。
discussion.en: Community vote: C (81%), with the remainder displayed as Other. Discussion noted the seven-day RDS stop limit, making snapshot retention plus deletion of compute the cheapest monthly-idle approach while restoring the original size when needed.
